action.reasoning: Observation: prediction-time blending of existing checkpoints did not beat the best single checkpoint; hidden-test IC fell from 0.00736 to 0.00557, while local validation remained higher. Analysis: the issue is likely not inference calibration alone but model objective mismatch. For IC, directly optimizing cross-sectional ranking/classification of next-day returns often generalizes better than raw regression. We already have a decent tree ensemble pipeline and enough budget for one targeted retrain. A pragmatic next step is to train a binary classifier on the sign of next-day return using the same cross-sectional preprocessing, then use predicted probability as score; this often improves rank-based metrics and can also help Pearson IC if scores are monotone with expected return. To reduce risk, keep the existing regression ensemble checkpoint intact and add a new lightweight classification ensemble checkpoint plus backward-compatible predict support. Decision: extend model.py/train.py/predict.py to support classifier ensembles (LightGBM binary + CatBoost classifier + logistic regression), train on 2008-2020, validate on 2021, locally test predict.py, and submit the new checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --ckpt_dir checkpoints/classifier_cs --task classification --num_boost_round 300 && python predict.py --data_path tmp_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/classifier_cs --output tmp_eval/out7.json
